description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who has just finished running a small
    inference-only benchmark locally.  
    Your goal is to persist the benchmark *artifact* in an
    easily parsable way so that downstream automation can pick it up.

    1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/mlops/benchmarks  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Inside that directory, create **exactly one** CSV file whose name
       must be
       perf_20240101T120000Z.csv  

       2.1  The file has three data rows and *must* follow **this exact format**
            (including lower-case header names, comma placement and
            number of decimal places):

            metric,value,unit
            latency_mean,123.45,ms
            latency_p99,200.00,ms
            throughput,50.00,req/s

            • The header line comes first.  
            • There are no extra blank lines or spaces.  
            • Every numeric value is fixed to two decimal places.

    3. Append (do **not** overwrite) the literal file name
       perf_20240101T120000Z.csv
       followed by a single newline character to
       /home/user/mlops/benchmark_index.txt.  
       If benchmark_index.txt does not exist, create it; if it does, leave any
       existing lines intact and only add the new entry as the last line.

    4. Print the entire contents of
       /home/user/mlops/benchmarks/perf_20240101T120000Z.csv
       to STDOUT so that the CI system can capture and display the benchmark
       summary.

    When you are finished, the directory tree and files should look like this:

    /home/user/mlops/
    ├── benchmark_index.txt        (now includes the filename as the last line)
    └── benchmarks/
        └── perf_20240101T120000Z.csv   (with the exact contents shown above)

    The automated grader will verify:

    • The directory /home/user/mlops/benchmarks exists.  
    • The CSV file exists with *exactly* the four lines shown, byte-for-byte.  
    • benchmark_index.txt exists and its last line is the literal string
      “perf_20240101T120000Z.csv”.  
    • Your final console output matches the CSV contents exactly.

    Hint: Achieving this requires no more than 3-4 shell commands.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth state after successful completion -->
  <file path="/home/user/mlops/benchmarks/perf_20240101T120000Z.csv"><![CDATA[
metric,value,unit
latency_mean,123.45,ms
latency_p99,200.00,ms
throughput,50.00,req/s
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/mlops/benchmark_index.txt">
    <!-- file may have pre-existing lines; grader will only check that the last line is exactly the filename -->
  </file>
name: task_000000_9a7f37fe
